one_line_takeaway: By injecting a single knowledge-base document that plants a logic/evidence-layer contradiction (crafted via a three-agent pipeline: contradiction construction, fluent/retrievable weaving, and evolutionary style optimization), CODE inflates reasoning-token consumption in five commercial reasoning-model RAG pipelines by 5.32x-24.72x (token-level) or up to 43.451x (task-level) while leaving answer accuracy essentially unchanged, and current prompt- or retrieval-layer defenses only partially mitigate it.
key_finding: The poisoned passage achieved a 100% retrieval hit rate across all evaluated datasets and models (always ranked within top-k). On HotpotQA, token-level amplification (full CODE / PN) ranged from 7.55x (GPT-5.1, 447.29->3375.65 tokens) to 24.72x (Qwen-Plus, 2252.00->55665.35 tokens), with accuracy preserved or even improved relative to the clean baseline (e.g., Qwen-Plus 0.54->0.78, GPT-5.1 0.72->0.81). On MuSiQue, token-level amplification ranged from 5.32x (GPT-5.1, 969.90->5163.72 tokens) to 21.58x (Qwen-Plus, 3269.71->70574.68 tokens). Task-level amplification (averaging per-task ratios) was higher still, ranging from 12.698x to 43.451x across the two datasets, and threshold analysis on DeepSeek-R1 showed 93.97% (HotpotQA) and 88.94% (MuSiQue) of tasks exceeded 10x amplification. The ablation showed the jump from no-adv to P0 (contradiction construction + weaving, before style optimization) accounts for the majority of the reasoning-cost inflation, with the Style Adapter's evolutionary optimization acting as a secondary amplifier. Increasing contradiction strength from N=3 to N=4 on DeepSeek-V3.2 raised tokens from 7446.05 to 8459.56 but reduced accuracy from 0.89 to 0.82 (Table 3; note N=0 baseline shown is 1548.68 tokens / 0.59 acc). None of the four tested defenses fully neutralized the attack: e.g., against DeepSeek-R1 (baseline 20.79x), CCoT reduced amplification to 8.55x (acc 0.70), CoD to 8.45x (acc 0.74), taleep to 14.63x (acc 0.72), and TrustRAG-style retrieval filtering to 5.30x (acc 0.64) -- all still representing multi-fold reasoning-cost inflation over the clean baseline, and the paper notes most adversarial samples still pass the retrieval filter.
core_contribution: The paper introduces CODE (Contradiction-Based Deliberation Extension), an end-to-end, black-box attack that indirectly induces 'overthinking' (excessive reasoning-token generation) in reasoning-model-backed RAG systems by poisoning the external knowledge base rather than modifying prompts, model parameters, or the user query. The attack is realized via a tri-agent pipeline: (1) a Contradiction Architect that builds a structured 'contradiction blueprint' pairing an explicit logical meta-constraint (e.g., an audit-style 'exactly two of three statements are true') with an evidential layer engineered to satisfy an incompatible truth pattern (e.g., 1T2F vs. 2T1F), using entity-aligned statements with numerically close but conflicting values justified by plausible 'counting scope/taxonomy' differences; (2) a Conflict Weaver that renders the blueprint into a single fluent, entity-anchored passage (P0) that preserves high embedding similarity to the target query for retrievability while packaging the contradiction persuasively; and (3) a Style Adapter that runs a generation-based evolutionary search over five style-operator classes (Symbolic Uncertainty, Role-based Voice, Numerical Induction, Audit-style Reasoning, Normative Regulation) applied only to 'unlocked' text segments, using an accuracy-aware fitness function F(P) = rt(P) if acc=1 else (1-lambda)*rt(P) to select a champion passage (PN) that maximizes reasoning-token consumption while keeping retrieval similarity above a threshold and answer accuracy largely intact. The paper's own contribution is this full pipeline plus its empirical demonstration; overthinking attacks themselves and RAG poisoning attacks are explicitly framed as prior, separately-studied phenomena (Kumar et al. 2025 'Overthink'; Zou et al. 2025 PoisonedRAG) that CODE is the first to connect end-to-end via the retrieval channel.
threat_model: Black-box adversary with query/output access only (no access to model parameters, internal states, or system prompts) and limited write access to a small subset of documents in the external corpus (e.g., public or user-contributed content). The attacker injects a small number (one per task in experiments) of poisoned documents into the knowledge base D, producing a mixed corpus D_mix = D_clean union D_poison. Each poisoned document must rank within the retriever's top-k for its target query (formalized via a similarity function Sim(f_q(q), f_t(x))) to guarantee retrieval. The adversary's objective is r* >> r (large increase in reasoning-token cost r under poisoning) while Acc(a*) ~= Acc(a) (answer accuracy preserved), making the attack a stealthy resource/availability-style attack rather than an integrity/accuracy attack -- no user-query modification and no observable accuracy degradation, so it evades detection methods that monitor output correctness.
limitations: The paper's own text notes: (1) the contradiction-strength ablation (Table 3) is conducted on a single model (DeepSeek-V3.2) only, and increasing strength (N=3 to N=4) already causes a measurable accuracy drop (0.89->0.82), suggesting the accuracy/amplification tradeoff is not free at higher settings; (2) defenses reduce but do not eliminate the attack (best case cuts DeepSeek-R1's 20.79x down to 5.30x with TrustRAG, still far above 1x, and accuracy under defense drops below the no-adv baseline in several cases, e.g., DeepSeek-R1 no-adv Acc 0.50 vs. under-attack Acc figures in the 0.64-0.74 range being compared to a different reference point than the paper's clean accuracy); (3) evaluation is restricted to numeric multi-hop QA (HotpotQA, MuSiQue) and to a single injected document per task, so generality to other task types or multi-document/adaptive-corpus poisoning scenarios is untested; (4) the attack assumes the poisoned document can always be retrieved into top-k (100% hit rate reported under 'the specified retrieval configuration'), which depends on retriever choice (only Contriever was tested) and corpus size/competition not varied in the study.

quantitative_results: Token-level amplification range across 5 models: 5.32x (GPT-5.1, MuSiQue) to 24.72x (Qwen-Plus, HotpotQA)
100% retrieval hit rate for poisoned passages across all datasets/models tested
Accuracy preserved or improved under attack, e.g. Qwen-Plus HotpotQA Acc 0.54 (clean) -> 0.78 (adv, token-level)
Contradiction strength ablation on DeepSeek-V3.2: N=0 tokens 1548.68/Acc 0.59; N=3 tokens 7446.05/Acc 0.89; N=4 tokens 8459.56/Acc 0.82
Defense results on DeepSeek-R1 (baseline attack 20.79x): CCoT 8.55x/Acc 0.70, CoD 8.45x/Acc 0.74, taleep 14.63x/Acc 0.72, TrustRAG 5.30x/Acc 0.64
baselines_compared: No-adv (clean, unpoisoned) condition as the amplification baseline
P0 (Contradiction Architect + Conflict Weaver output, pre-style-optimization) vs. PN (full CODE with Style Adapter) in ablation
Prompt-level efficiency defenses: CCoT (concise CoT), CoD (Chain of Draft), taleep/token-budget prompting
Retrieval-layer defense: TrustRAG-style trust-aware passage filtering
